title: Plugin chain P1: scaffold the Claude Code plugin (manifest + marketplace + component relocation)
description:
First ticket of the plugin packaging chain (epic TASK-020). Stand up the plugin skeleton per `tasks/TASK-020.research.md` §A. Create `.claude-plugin/plugin.json` (name `agentic-framework`, `mcpServers: "./.mcp.json"`, `version` left unset during dev for commit-SHA versioning per §A.2) and `.claude-plugin/marketplace.json` (same-repo single-plugin layout, `source: "./"`, per §A.3). Relocate the four agents and the skills to the **plugin root** `agents/` and `skills/` (NOT under `.claude/` — the plugin loader expects them at root, §A.4). Add a `commands/` dir. Per the human decision, **exclude `bin/make-template.js` from the shipped plugin `bin/`** (it's a publish-time dev scrub tool; keep it in the repo for publish use only). **Critical constraint — do not break THIS repo's own agent usage.** This repo is simultaneously the framework's dev environment (it uses `.claude/agents/orchestrator|developer|reviewer|researcher.md` right now) AND the plugin source. A naive move of `.claude/agents/` → `agents/` would strip the dev repo of the agents we use to build it. The scaffold must preserve dev-repo agent availability — e.g. keep the dev repo loadable via `claude --plugin-dir .` (self-install), or retain a dev copy, or document the dev workflow change. Resolve this explicitly; do not silently break it. **Verification uses the non-interactive CLI** confirmed in §D.5: `claude plugin validate`, `claude plugin install

@`, `claude plugin details`, `claude plugin uninstall`.
acceptance_criteria: `.claude-plugin/plugin.json` exists with name `agentic-framework` and `mcpServers: "./.mcp.json"`; `claude plugin validate <repo>` passes (warnings OK, no errors).
`.claude-plugin/marketplace.json` exists with `source: "./"` and a valid `owner`; validate passes.

The four agents live at plugin-root `agents/` and the skills at plugin-root `skills/`. Installing from a local marketplace and running `claude plugin details agentic-framework` lists Agents (4) and the skills with zero manifest errors. (Document the exact commands + observed inventory in the ticket close.)
`bin/make-template.js` is NOT part of the shipped plugin `bin/`; `bin/init.js` and `bin/new-task.js` are. A test or documented check confirms the shipped bin set.

Dev-repo agent usage is preserved via the locked 'keep both + drift-guard' strategy: `.claude/agents/` stays intact as the live dev source of truth (bare names, so this session keeps spawning developer/reviewer/researcher), AND plugin-root `agents/` holds copies for shipping. A `tests/agents-parity.spec.js` test FAILS if the two directories diverge (same set of agent files, byte-identical contents). `.claude/agents/` is NOT deleted.
Throwaway install artifacts are cleaned up (uninstall + marketplace remove) so the verification leaves no residue in user plugin config; chain follow-ups can re-run the same sensor.

comments.author: orchestrator
comments.body: Minted from TASK-020 spike §F (P1) + §A. Foundation ticket — no deps. The dev-repo-agent-preservation constraint (AC5) is the non-obvious risk: don't break the agents we use to build the framework while relocating them to the plugin layout.

comments.author: orchestrator
comments.body: Human decision (AC5): 'keep both + drift-guard'. .claude/agents/ remains the live dev source of truth so subagent spawning keeps working through TASK-022..027 in the active session; plugin-root agents/ gets byte-identical copies for shipping; tests/agents-parity.spec.js guards drift. Rejected self-load (--plugin-dir .) because it would namespace the agents and require relaunching THIS session before the chain could continue. Skills: relocate only the FRAMEWORK's OWN skills under .claude/skills/ (verify what is actually repo-local vs user-global before moving — do NOT sweep global gsd-*/etc. skills into the plugin).

comments.author: developer
comments.body: IMPL complete (commits d84b96b test + 61aa366 impl). Created .claude-plugin/{plugin.json,marketplace.json,shipped-bin.json}, plugin-root agents/ (4 byte-identical copies — authored by orchestrator after a Bash cp permission block; byte-identity guarded by agents-parity.spec.js), skills/tech-training-template/SKILL.md. 224 passed / 1 skipped twice, deterministic, zero regressions. plugin.json: name agentic-framework, mcpServers ./.mcp.json, no version. shipped-bin.json excludes make-template.js. .claude/agents/ untouched; orchestrator.md v1 text left for P5.

comments.author: orchestrator
comments.body: CLI sensor (orchestrator-run, AC1/2/3/6 proof on the REAL plugin): `claude plugin validate .` → passed; marketplace add (abs path) + install agentic-framework@agentic-framework-marketplace → succeeded; `claude plugin details agentic-framework` → Agents (4): developer, orchestrator, researcher, reviewer; Skills (1): tech-training-template; MCP servers (0), no errors; uninstall + marketplace remove → zero residue. Finding: `marketplace add .` is rejected (needs ./path or abs path) — abs path works; note for P7 docs. Dangling mcpServers ref tolerated (file lands in P6).

comments.author: reviewer
comments.body: Verdict (fresh context): APPROVE. 0 HIGH / 0 MEDIUM / 1 LOW / 1 NIT. 224 passed + 1 skipped, twice, deterministic; 206 pre-existing green (no regressions). Tests-first confirmed (git diff 61aa366^..61aa366 -- tests/ empty). All 6 ACs COVERED with named tests. AC5 parity independently verified: all 4 agent pairs share identical git blob hashes (real Buffer.equals comparison), .claude/agents/ preserved → session subagents survive the chain. No scope creep (skills/ holds only tech-training-template; orchestrator.md v1 text untouched, P5's territory). LOW: dangling mcpServers ./.mcp.json ref — correctly DEFERRED to P6 (mandated by AC1, CLI tolerated it). NIT: empty references/ subdir in the source skill not copied — harmless (zero content); revisit only if later populated. No HIGH/MEDIUM — nothing blocks close.

comments.author: orchestrator
comments.body: Closed on reviewer APPROVE under standing auto-close authorization for the plugin chain (human-granted). Linked commits d84b96b (test) + 61aa366 (impl). LOW (mcpServers) tracked by TASK-026/P6; NIT (empty references/) deferred without follow-up. P1 done — plugin loads, validates, installs, registers Agents=4. Proceeding to TASK-022 (P2 retargeting).
